problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: Dopamine/serotonin reuptake inhibition with specificity may resolve Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? by blocking at the systems level.

experiment.protocol: Optogenetic mouse model with 3 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
